hq: 2261 Market Street, STE 10960, San Francisco, CA 94114 US

system_description.overview: Olive Financial, Inc. is an AI-native company that provides an AI-powered internal tool builder designed to help organizations create, maintain, and deploy internal software tools without extensive manual coding. The platform leverages natural language processing to automatically generate functional internal tools, enabling organizations to efficiently manage data and gain actionable insights.

products.AI-powered internal tool builder: A platform that enables organizations to create internal tools connected to their data with natural language, and share them with their teams.

infrastructure.cloud_provider: AWS (carve-out; AWS controls excluded from scope)
infrastructure.physical_presence: None - no physical offices; all data processed on cloud services
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (confirmed: spinning up multiple servers across all availability zones in AWS; backups taken daily with multi-AZ configured)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Segmented VPCs with access control lists (ACLs). Network segmentation implemented to separate systems and data based on sensitivity and criticality.
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on the application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via AWS to identify and protect against threats in conjunction with security policy network settings.
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access via bastion host with VPN
network_architecture.tls: TLS using certificates from an established third-party certificate authority for all web-based user requests
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routing through proxy server; email scanning to prevent malicious scripts and viruses
network_architecture.topology: Users/Web Browser -> CloudFront CDN -> API Gateway -> Lambda/EC2 -> ECS Services -> Databases. Separate CodeBuild/CodePipeline CI/CD pipeline. CloudFormation for IaC. Bastion host for remote admin access via VPN.

application_architecture.pattern: Hybrid (Lambda serverless + ECS containers visible in network diagram)
application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments (confirmed: CC8.1.4 - changes developed and tested in separate environment before implementation)

databases: Not specifically named; database references throughout but no specific DB engine disclosed

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: AWS IAM / Password policy of AWS
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for user accounts with administrative access to production platform; MFA enforced for all remote access; MFA required for source code tool access
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Controlled through AWS Password policy with periodic forced changes, password expiry, and complexity requirements. Accounts disabled after limited unsuccessful logon attempts.

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service (AWS KMS implied)
encryption.key_management: Cloud provider key management service; access to encryption keys restricted to authorized personnel only

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Source code management tool with MFA access (specific tool not named; likely GitHub based on CodePipeline integration visible in diagram)
ci_cd_devops.ci_cd_tool: AWS CodeBuild / AWS CodePipeline (visible in network diagram)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Repository branch rules configured to ensure every merge request to move change to production requires additional approval. Developers cannot make changes to production code without additional approval.
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management with monthly management reviews. CTO/Management performs monthly patch management reviews. Workstations configured for automatic updates and scanned daily for patch compliance.

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not named; monitoring tool generates alerts when thresholds are met
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Not named; logging enabled to monitor administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions at application and infrastructure level
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured to notify IT management of high and critical risk events

third_party_services.purpose: Cloud infrastructure and data center services (subservice organization)

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and Malware protection software installed on all production servers and Mac-based workstations/laptops. Auto-scan on continuous basis. Definitions updated weekly for production servers, daily for workstations.
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications, frequency adjusted as required. Self-assessment scan prior to every release or yearly.
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party. Results reviewed by IT management. High/critical findings remediated and added to risk register.

untestable: CC2.2.3 - No significant changes to people, roles and responsibilities occurred during the engagement period
CC3.2.1 (Cybersecurity insurance) - No cyber incidents reported during the engagement period
CC6.5.2 (Customer data deletion upon termination) - No customer terminations during the engagement period
CC7.3.1 (Incident response) - No security incidents reported during the engagement period
excluded_criteria: CC6.4 - Physical access to facilities: AWS is responsible for physical security of data centers; not in scope
A1.2 - Environmental protections: Olive does not have an office; managed by building management/AWS
Processing Integrity - Determined not relevant to the system
Privacy - Determined not relevant to the system
notable_controls: BCDR policy documented and reviewed annually with annual testing of loss scenarios (CC7.4.1)
Backups taken daily with multi-AZ configured and backup failure alerts (CC7.4.2)
Risk and Governance Executive Committee (RGEC) meets semiannually to review internal controls (CC2.1.2)
IT Leadership Committee meets weekly to review security activities and performance metrics (CC3.1.1)
Configuration management tool used for IaC with rollback capability (CC7.1.1)
Formal vendor management with quarterly risk assessment for critical vendors without SOC reports (CC9.2.1)

infrastructure.rationale: AWS-only with Multi-AZ, CloudFront CDN, and IaC via CloudFormation. Network diagram shows solid AWS architecture with Lambda, ECS, EC2, S3, CodePipeline. However, single region (no multi-region), no named regions specified, and the report lacks detail on specific configurations. Deduction for no multi-region and limited specificity.

application_architecture.rationale: Hybrid pattern with Lambda, ECS, and EC2 is reasonable. Branch protection and code review enforced. However, no programming languages, frameworks, or API styles are disclosed. The report provides very little technical depth on the application layer itself.

data_layer.rationale: Daily backups with multi-AZ, data classification policy, retention policy, and per-tenant segregation are positives. However, no specific database engines, caching layers, or data warehouse are named. S3 visible in diagram but the data layer is largely undisclosed.

security_posture.rationale: Strong security fundamentals: MFA enforced, IDS/IPS via AWS, annual pen testing by third party, monthly vulnerability scanning, endpoint protection with antivirus, firewalls with annual review, server hardening, cybersecurity insurance. Data encryption at rest and in transit. Log protection and access restriction on logs. Missing explicit WAF and named SIEM/monitoring tools.

devops_maturity.rationale: AWS CodePipeline/CodeBuild for CI/CD, CloudFormation for IaC, ECS for container orchestration, branch protection requiring approval for production merges, separate environments, emergency change process. Source code management with MFA. Good but tools are mostly inferred from the network diagram rather than explicitly described.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented and reviewed annually, annual testing of loss scenarios, daily backups with multi-AZ, multi-AZ failover by spinning up servers across all AZs. However, no stated RTO/RPO, no multi-region failover, and limited detail on specific recovery procedures.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Extreme concentration on AWS as the sole named infrastructure vendor. No other third-party services are named in the report. The vendor management policy exists but the actual vendor ecosystem is opaque. Single critical dependency on AWS.

overall.rationale: Olive presents a solid compliance posture with zero exceptions across 55 controls tested, unqualified opinion, and good foundational security practices. The AWS infrastructure with Multi-AZ, IaC, CI/CD pipeline, and container orchestration shows reasonable maturity. However, the report is notably thin on technical specifics: no named databases, languages, frameworks, monitoring tools, or SIEM. Heavy AWS concentration with no multi-region. The 3-month audit period is very short. For an AI-native company, the lack of disclosed technology depth is a concern for tech due diligence.

red_flags: Extreme vendor concentration: AWS is the only named infrastructure vendor with no disclosed alternatives or multi-region strategy
Very short audit period (3 months: May-July 2025) suggests this is likely their first SOC 2 audit
No RTO/RPO disclosed despite having BCDR policy
No specific database technologies named despite handling customer data
No named monitoring/APM/SIEM tools - only generic references to 'monitoring tools'
yellow_flags: No WAF mentioned despite being a web application platform
Processing Integrity and Privacy trust criteria excluded as 'not relevant' - unusual for a platform that processes customer data and generates tools with natural language
No specific programming languages or frameworks disclosed - unusual for an engineering-focused AI company
Cybersecurity insurance control untestable because no incidents occurred - policy existence was confirmed but not the claims process
Several controls untestable due to no events during the short engagement period (no terminations, no incidents, no significant personnel changes)
green_flags: Clean unqualified opinion with zero exceptions across all tested controls
MFA enforced across production platform, remote access, and source code management
Daily backups with multi-AZ configuration and alerts for backup failures
Annual penetration testing by independent third party with remediation tracking
Formal change management with branch protection requiring approval before production deployment
BCDR policy with annual testing across different loss scenarios
Quarterly user access reviews including administrative accounts
One business day SLA for access revocation upon termination
Data classification policy established and documented
IT Leadership Committee meets weekly; RGEC meets semiannually for governance
Network diagram provided showing production AWS architecture
Infrastructure as Code via CloudFormation with rollback capability
key_observations: The network diagram (page 18) reveals significantly more architecture detail than the text: Lambda, API Gateway, CloudFront, CodeBuild, CodePipeline, ECS, EC2, S3, CloudFormation are all visible but never discussed in the narrative - suggesting the report was prepared from a template with minimal customization of the technical sections

For an AI-native company, there is zero mention of AI/ML infrastructure, model serving, GPU compute, or any AI-specific technology stack - the system description only references 'natural language processing' at a high level
The report references 'Windows/Network team' for patch management (page 19) which seems incongruent with a cloud-native AI startup - this may be boilerplate from the audit firm
Olive operates with no physical office and all cloud-based - this is a very lean/remote operation typical of an early-stage startup
The org chart shows only CEO -> CTO -> Engineering Team, suggesting a very small company
The report was signed on 8/19/2025, just 19 days after the audit period ended on 7/31/2025 - unusually fast turnaround
Per-tenant data segregation (CC6.1.3) is a positive signal for enterprise readiness
